title: Recruiter Screens, Written Communication, and Process Control · Cumulative Campaign Review
summary: A delayed hiring review reconstructs recruiter screens, written communication, and process control from target, claim, evidence, audience, action, observation, changed case, rejected failure, repair, consent, owner, and next-decision evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for screening conversation and process record after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Deliver a flexible evidence-grounded introduction connecting target work, relevant behavior, proof, and reason for this conversation.
Clarify ambiguous role, level, process, location, schedule, compensation, work sample, and decision criteria before answering.
Respond to irrelevant, sensitive, or jurisdiction-dependent questions by protecting privacy, redirecting to job function, and seeking qualified local guidance when needed.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Screen Close
steps.instruction:
review case — A rushed screen mixes role evidence, pay, location, authorization, access, sensitive history, unclear process, and an implied but unconfirmed next step. The review variation 2 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Confirm mutual interest, unresolved questions, next step, owner, timing, artifact promises, contact channel, and follow-up record.

Inspect the Screen Close trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Screen Close.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for confirm mutual interest, unresolved questions, next step, owner, timing, artifact promises, contact channel, and follow-up record. For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Screen Close, keep Screen Close connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: For Screen Close, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Screen Close, name the learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, truthful claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer boundary, Screen Close precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Separate evidence from confidence · Review Screen Close, trace this concrete input and predict targeting, source, evidence, contribution, access, privacy, consent, process, decision, rejection, repair, follow-up, and learner-controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: A rushed screen mixes role evidence, pay, location, authorization, access, sensitive history, unclear process, and an implied but unconfirmed next step. The review variation 2 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Confirm mutual interest, unresolved questions, next step, owner, timing, artifact promises, contact channel, and follow-up record.

For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Screen Close, challenge the documented misconception with changed-candidate, target-role, source-date, jurisdiction, audience, access, privacy, consent, process-state, interview, offer, rejection, repair, and learner-controlled transfer evidence: A friendly conversation implies advancement.

stimulus.title: Screen Close case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A rushed screen mixes role evidence, pay, location, authorization, access, sensitive history, unclear process, and an implied but unconfirmed next step. The review variation 2 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Confirm mutual interest, unresolved questions, next step, owner, timing, artifact promises, contact channel, and follow-up record.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Confirm mutual interest, unresolved questions, next step, owner, timing, artifact promises, contact channel, and follow-up record. Artifact under review: screening conversation and process record.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Screen Close, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim fails the evidence boundary for Screen Close.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Concise Introduction
steps.instruction:
Order the Concise Introduction evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Concise Introduction.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Concise Introduction assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Concise Introduction, keep Concise Introduction connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

steps.title: Record what required effort · Review Screen Close
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Screen Close: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A rushed screen mixes role evidence, pay, location, authorization, access, sensitive history, unclear process, and an implied but unconfirmed next step. The review variation 4 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Confirm mutual interest, unresolved questions, next step, owner, timing, artifact promises, contact channel, and follow-up record.

Evidence target: Record what required effort · Review Screen Close.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Record what required effort · Review Screen Close, keep Screen Close connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Confirm mutual interest, unresolved questions, next step, owner, timing, artifact promises, contact channel, and follow-up record.
